title: Probability of Backtest Overfitting (PBO) at the Loop A promotion gate
source: GitHub repo scout (4 Haiku explorers) + quant-phd-advisor literature review, 2026-05-31. Conversation: 'search quant trading repositories'.
why: DSR deflates a Sharpe for the NUMBER of trials; PBO (via CSCV) directly estimates P(in-sample-best config underperforms OOS median) — a complementary angle on SELECTION overfit. quant-phd-advisor: PBO is the natural metric for an autonomous loop that manufactures many configs (primaries x regimes x params) — exactly the selection process PBO was built to police — and it operates on the config family the loop already produces, sidestepping per-fold trade starvation.
value_rationale: VERDICT: Adopt-with-caveats, scoped to the LOOP not a single run. Best fit to the actual risk (loop selection overfit), genuinely complementary to DSR.
scope: Compute PBO/CSCV across the loop's survivor config family at the promotion gate
CRITICAL: PBO needs a strategy FAMILY (N>=~8-16 comparable configs) to be meaningful; on one frozen pipeline run it is near-vacuous
Belongs at scripts/loop_a_tick.py / proposal-audit promotion gate, NOT pipeline/stack.py's per-run gate
recommendation: Adopt at the loop gate. Skip standalone permutation tests as low-value — they mainly catch leakage the purge/embargo/.shift(1) invariants already prevent.
out_of_scope: Not a per-fold or per-run metric. Standalone Monte-Carlo permutation tests: low value here (redundant with existing leakage invariants).

external_refs.label: Probability of Backtest Overfitting (Bailey et al. 2017)
